subject: introverts
one_liner: The business bank you operate in complete silence.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan, banking-grade seriousness applied to total social withdrawal. Never winks. Treats avoiding all human contact as a premium financial feature, audited and SOC 2 compliant.

hero.h1: The business bank you never have to talk to.
hero.sub: Burrow is a full business account you run in complete silence. No branch. No phone tree. No relationship manager who just wanted to put a face to the name.

features.title: Voicemail, all the way down
features.blurb: Every line rings to voicemail, including ours. We transcribe it, resolve it, and never call back. You will not hear a human voice for the duration of our relationship.

features.title: The Decline button
features.blurb: One tap turns any call, meeting, or quick sync into a wire transfer. The money arrives on time. You do not arrive at all.

features.title: Alerts you can sense
features.blurb: Low balance? Burrow does not call, text, or buzz. The interface dims by four percent and goes quiet. You will know, and no one will have spoken.

pricing_tiers.name: Mute
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the sole proprietor who left the group chat in 2019.
perks: Statements from an address marked no-reply
A debit card with your initials, if you insist

pricing_tiers.name: Airplane Mode
pricing_tiers.tagline: For teams that have never once been in the same room.
perks: Up to five users, none of whom have met
Approvals handled entirely inside a shared doc
Branch access disabled at signup, permanently

pricing_tiers.name: Witness Protection
pricing_tiers.tagline: For founders who left no forwarding address.
perks: An account number we will not confirm exists
A relationship manager you are forbidden from contacting
We tell your investors you are heads down

testimonials.quote: Two years with Burrow and I have spoken to no one. My accountant assumes I have passed. Revenue is up nineteen percent.
testimonials.name: Dana Reuter
testimonials.title: Sole Proprietor of a Firm She Declines to Name

testimonials.quote: They closed my local branch because I asked them to. A card arrived in the mail. I have not opened the envelope.
testimonials.name: Marcus Veld
testimonials.title: Cofounder, Two-Person Logistics Company (One Is a Spreadsheet)

testimonials.quote: Burrow declined a call from my own CFO and wired him the amount instead. We have never communicated more clearly.
testimonials.name: Priya Anand
testimonials.title: CEO, Series A, Camera Permanently Off

demo_screen_concept: A quiet business-banking dashboard built around a single oversized Do Not Disturb toggle. The top shows a Silence Score: the number of human interactions Burrow absorbed on your behalf this month (calls intercepted, meetings declined, syncs converted to wires). Below sits a calm account balance in tabular figures, a ledger of recent transactions, and a Contact Requests inbox where each pending human (an accountant, a CFO, a partnerships email) has exactly one button: Decline and Wire. Toggling Do Not Disturb visibly dims the entire interface and freezes all incoming requests. A filter labeled transactions that required talking to a person returns, always, nothing.

design_direction.style: Hushed quiet-minimalism, low-stimulation calm mode. A warm fog-grey ground carries a single narrow column of content with enormous, deliberate whitespace, as if the page itself is keeping its voice down. Type is set small and calm rather than oversized: no shouting headlines, no loud blocks, no decorative ornament. One muted slate-blue accent is rationed strictly for the single primary CTA, the live Do Not Disturb status dot, and the quiet underline on the one link that matters, and appears almost nowhere else. Everything else is graphite ink on fog. Section dividers are a single hairline rule the color of the muted tone. Figures that pretend to be money (balances, the Silence Score, tier prices, disbursement totals) are set in the body face with tabular figures, deliberately not a loud console monospace, so the numbers stay as hushed as the rest of the page. Motion is almost absent: content fades in slowly over 600ms on load, hover states shift opacity by a few percent and nothing moves, the Do Not Disturb toggle slides without a sound. No gradients beyond a faint paper vignette, no glassmorphism, no neon, no hard offset shadows, no bento tiles, no ornament, no dark mode. Reads like a well-funded business bank that has been engineered, with total seriousness, around the premise that the customer would prefer the entire financial system simply leave them alone.

fonts.body: 'Work Sans', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

design_direction.archetype: Minimal single-column fintech landing rendered as a deliberately quiet page: a hushed hero with a small literary-serif wordmark and a calm one-line balance plate, three text-led feature rows separated by hairline rules, three understated pull-quote testimonials with quiet initials in place of headshots, a three-tier pricing rack styled as account classes, a single rationed slate-blue CTA, and a demo handoff to the Burrow Quiet Dashboard.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Near-absent. Slow 600ms fade-in on load; hover changes opacity only, no transforms; the Do Not Disturb toggle slides smoothly; respects prefers-reduced-motion by disabling all of it.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Single centered column on a wide fog field. Small serif wordmark top-left, a one-line nav with a single CTA top-right. Headline set calm and medium-sized, not oversized. One line of sub-copy. A single quiet account-balance plate below the CTA in tabular figures. Most of the hero is intentional empty space.

images.rationale: All four image slots skipped by design, not by cap or missing key. Hero: skipped because the day's style is hushed typographic quiet-minimalism with no imagery role. Demo subject assets: skipped because the demo is a console/dashboard (Do Not Disturb toggle, ledger, contact-request inbox), which is not photo-shaped. Testimonial headshots: skipped on purpose because the introvert theme rejects faces, and the quiet-minimalist layout uses quiet initials instead of photos. An entirely image-free, text-led page is the intended outcome here.
